attack_paths.description: User-supplied input flows into SQL and NoSQL query strings without parameterization, allowing unauthenticated attackers to bypass the login check, dump the entire user table, and overwrite arbitrary product reviews.

The RSA private key used to sign all session tokens is committed to the public repository, and the JWT library accepts unsigned tokens, so anyone who reads the source can mint a forged administrator token without touching the login endpoint.

The registration and profile-update endpoints accept a role attribute in the request body and write it to the database without restricting which fields a caller may set, enabling any visitor to self-promote to administrator.

Two server-side routes evaluate attacker-controlled input as JavaScript — the user-profile handler via eval() and the B2B order endpoint via a sandbox that is escapable through prototype pollution — giving an authenticated attacker arbitrary code execution inside the container.

Encryption keys, FTP directory contents, access logs, and stack traces are served without authentication on predictable URL paths, and a server-side request forgery sink lets an attacker redirect the server to fetch internal resources.

Product descriptions and search results render attacker-supplied HTML without encoding, and session tokens are held in JavaScript-readable browser storage with no content security policy, so any XSS payload immediately yields the victim's session token.
